sources.title: Primary Mortgage Market Survey
sources.claimSupported: National 30-year and 15-year fixed-rate benchmark context, not a personalized or local quote.
sources.limitation: National survey averages are not Snap offers, APRs, personalized rates, local quotes, or borrower-specific payment terms.

sources.title: FHFA House Price Index Monthly Report, June 2026
sources.geographicScope: FHFA HPI geographies including state, MSA, county, ZIP Code, and tract where available
sources.claimSupported: Dated home-price trend context that must retain its published geography.
sources.limitation: The repeat-sales index is not a median sale price, listing inventory measure, city forecast, property valuation, or appraisal; recent observations may be revised.

sources.title: FHFA House Price Index Report, 2026 Q1
sources.geographicScope: Supported metropolitan statistical areas and divisions in the FHFA quarterly report
sources.claimSupported: Quarterly MSA home-price trend context through 2026 Q1; not a city-level listing measure or individual property valuation.
sources.limitation: The repeat-sales index must retain its published MSA or division and period; it is not city inventory, a median sale price, a forecast, or a property valuation.

sources.title: General Information on Project Standards
sources.geographicScope: United States conventional loans subject to Fannie Mae project standards
sources.claimSupported: Fannie Mae project eligibility and lender project-review framework; not a universal rule for every condo loan or buyer due-diligence question.
sources.limitation: This framework applies only to loans and projects subject to Fannie Mae standards; lender, product, project, and buyer due-diligence requirements may differ.

sources.title: Full Review Process
sources.geographicScope: United States conventional condo loans subject to Fannie Mae full project review
sources.claimSupported: Documents and project characteristics evaluated in Fannie Mae's full-review process; lender and product requirements must be confirmed for the proposed loan.
sources.limitation: The full-review process is not universal to every condo transaction; the proposed lender must confirm the applicable review path and current requirements.

sources.title: Self-Employment Documentation Requirements for an Individual
sources.geographicScope: United States conventional loans subject to Fannie Mae income documentation standards
sources.claimSupported: Fannie Mae's individual self-employment documentation framework; jumbo and lender-specific requirements require separate confirmation.
sources.limitation: The guidance applies to qualifying conventional loans under Fannie Mae standards and does not establish a universal jumbo or lender-specific document list.

sources.title: Self-Employment Documentation Requirements for a Business
sources.geographicScope: United States conventional loans subject to Fannie Mae business-income documentation standards
sources.claimSupported: Fannie Mae's business self-employment documentation framework; applicable documents depend on the borrower, business, loan, and lender review.
sources.limitation: The guidance applies to qualifying conventional loans under Fannie Mae standards; required records still depend on the borrower, business, transaction, and lender review.

sources.title: Employment Offers or Contracts
sources.geographicScope: United States conventional loans subject to Fannie Mae employment-income standards
sources.claimSupported: Fannie Mae's framework for qualifying income documented with an employment offer or contract; applicability and timing require lender confirmation.
sources.limitation: The framework applies only when current Fannie Mae requirements are met; the lender must confirm eligibility, documentation, timing, and all other underwriting conditions.

sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
sources.title: FHA Single Family Housing Policy Handbook 4000.1
sources.geographicScope: United States FHA Single Family Housing program
sources.claimSupported: Official FHA borrower, property, appraisal, mortgage-insurance, and lender policy framework, subject to the current handbook version and lender review.
sources.limitation: Current handbook provisions and incorporated updates control; the source does not guarantee approval, terms, property acceptance, or the absence of lender overlays.

sources.title: FHA Loans
sources.claimSupported: Borrower-facing FHA down-payment, mortgage-insurance, and loan-comparison guidance; final eligibility and terms require lender review.
sources.limitation: This is general consumer education, not a current lender offer, eligibility decision, mortgage-insurance quote, approval, or commitment to lend.

sources.title: Compare Loan Estimates
sources.claimSupported: Matched Loan Estimate comparison of lender-controlled costs, loan terms, monthly payments, prepaids, and initial escrow amounts.
sources.limitation: Comparisons require matching loan assumptions and dates; taxes and insurance are not lender-controlled, and the guidance is not a quote or Loan Estimate.

sources.title: Your Home Loan Toolkit
sources.claimSupported: Loan-offer comparison across interest rate, payment, closing costs, cash to close, and longer-term borrowing cost.
sources.limitation: The toolkit is general education and does not supply current pricing, a personalized comparison, a Loan Estimate, an eligibility result, or approval.

sources.title: FHFA Announces Conforming Loan Limit Values for 2026
sources.geographicScope: United States county and county-equivalent limits
sources.claimSupported: 2026 conforming baseline and high-cost loan-limit comparisons.
sources.limitation: National baseline and ceiling values are not a local county limit; local use requires the official county file and county-equivalent identifier, including special-area rules.

sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
sources.title: FHA Mortgage Limits Lookup
sources.claimSupported: FHA Forward loan limits that must be checked by county or MSA.
sources.limitation: National FHA floor and ceiling values cannot substitute for the applicable county or MSA limit; the current lookup and case-number effective rules control.

sources.title: VA Home Loans
sources.geographicScope: United States federal VA loan program
sources.claimSupported: VA guaranty, occupancy, Certificate of Eligibility, funding-fee, and program-path context.
sources.limitation: The overview does not establish an individual's eligibility, entitlement, fee, rate, approval, property acceptance, or lender terms; transaction-specific VA and lender review still applies.

sources.title: Owning a Home
sources.claimSupported: Home-shopping and mortgage-comparison education.
sources.limitation: This consumer education does not provide current lender pricing, property-specific costs, personalized loan options, eligibility, approval, or a commitment to lend.

sources.title: Loan Estimate
sources.claimSupported: Loan Estimate payment, APR, closing-cost, prepaid, escrow, and cash-to-close comparisons.
sources.limitation: An illustrative result is not a Loan Estimate, and CFPB attribution cannot support rates, fees, payments, or other scenario values that CFPB did not publish.

sources.title: Mortgage Key Terms
sources.claimSupported: Plain-language definitions for APR, ARM, amortization, income, and Closing Disclosure.
sources.limitation: Definitions provide general context only and do not establish transaction-specific calculations, current loan terms, eligibility, underwriting treatment, or approval.

sources.title: What is a jumbo loan?
sources.claimSupported: General jumbo-loan definition; current numeric limits come from FHFA.
sources.limitation: The page supports the general definition only; current county limits must come from FHFA, and jumbo pricing, documentation, reserves, and eligibility vary by lender.

sources.title: Local Area Unemployment Statistics
sources.dataPeriod: May 2026 observations; state release 2026-06-23 and metro release 2026-07-01
sources.claimSupported: Dated labor-market context, not a prediction of an individual borrower's eligibility.
sources.limitation: Use the exact LAUS series, geography, seasonal-adjustment status, and period; May state values were preliminary and do not establish household income or mortgage eligibility.

sources.title: Property Tax
sources.claimSupported: Texas has no state property tax; local taxing units set rates and bills.
sources.limitation: Local appraisal-district, taxing-unit, exemption, and property records control an actual Texas bill; statewide guidance does not provide a property-specific estimate.

sources.title: Residential Insurance
sources.claimSupported: Residential insurance shopping, FAIR Plan last-resort, and difference-in-conditions context.
sources.limitation: No property-specific availability, premium, coverage, eligibility, or insurability conclusion may be inferred; actual policy terms and licensed carrier review control.

sources.title: Property Taxation
sources.claimSupported: Colorado property-tax administration and assessment guidance.
sources.limitation: Current county valuation, classification, assessment rate, exemptions, mill levies, and an actual property bill require local records for the applicable tax year.

sources.title: Property Tax
sources.claimSupported: Florida property-tax administration and homestead framing.
sources.limitation: County and local property records, assessed value, millage, exemptions, and eligibility control an actual Florida tax obligation; statewide guidance is not an estimate.

sources.publisher: Federal Emergency Management Agency / National Flood Insurance Program
sources.title: FloodSmart
sources.claimSupported: Flood coverage limits in typical homeowners policies and flood-risk-map context.
sources.limitation: General flood education and map context do not determine address-specific risk, required coverage, policy terms, premium, availability, or a lender's flood determination.

sources.title: Homeowners Insurance
sources.claimSupported: General homeowners-insurance coverage structure and state-department lookup guidance.
sources.limitation: This is general consumer guidance; state law, the insurer, the actual policy, property characteristics, and licensed review control coverage, availability, and premium.

sources.title: 2024 ACS 5-Year Data, Austin city, Texas
sources.claimSupported: City-level survey estimates for median home value, selected monthly owner costs, and household income.
sources.limitation: These are 2020-2024 five-year survey estimates with 90% confidence margins of error, not January 2026 market observations, live listings, appraisals, or property valuations.

sources.claimSupported: City-level survey estimates for median home value, selected monthly owner costs, and household income.
sources.limitation: These are 2020-2024 five-year survey estimates with 90% confidence margins of error, not January 2026 market observations, live listings, appraisals, or property valuations.

sources.title: Property Tax System Basics
sources.dataPeriod: Current Texas property-tax system guidance accessed 2026-07-13
sources.claimSupported: County appraisal districts value property, local taxing units adopt rates, and multiple jurisdictions may tax the same parcel.
sources.limitation: The statewide system description does not calculate an actual bill; current appraisal, jurisdictions, adopted rates, exemptions, and property records must be checked locally.

sources.title: Property Tax Exemptions
sources.dataPeriod: Residence-homestead exemption amounts and guidance displayed 2026-07-13
sources.claimSupported: General and local-option residence-homestead exemption framework, application responsibility, and the $140,000 school-district exemption shown as of access.
sources.limitation: Qualification, filing, local-option exemptions, and the resulting property bill vary; the appraisal district and current local records control an owner's actual treatment.

sources.title: Valuing Property
sources.dataPeriod: Texas appraisal and value-limitation guidance, including 2026 parameters, accessed 2026-07-13
sources.claimSupported: Appraisal notices, residence-homestead appraised-value limitation, effective timing, and treatment of new improvements.
sources.limitation: The limitation depends on qualification, timing, ownership, and property changes; the appraisal district and current local records control the actual appraised value and bill.

sources.title: What is an escrow or impound account?
sources.claimSupported: Escrow accounts collect certain property expenses monthly, and changes in taxes or insurance can change the escrow and total mortgage payment.
sources.limitation: Escrow collection does not fix the underlying tax or insurance bill, guarantee sufficient funds, or provide a property-specific estimate; the servicer's analysis controls collection.

sources.title: California Property Tax: An Overview, Publication 29
sources.claimSupported: Proposition 13 rate framework, base-year assessment, annual value adjustments, county administration, and appeal context.
sources.limitation: The statewide overview does not determine a parcel's base-year value, voter-approved debt, exemptions, reassessment, or bill; county records and current law control.

sources.title: Supplemental Assessment
sources.claimSupported: Change-in-ownership supplemental assessments, proration, one- or two-bill timing, and direct delivery of supplemental bills to owners rather than lenders.
sources.limitation: An actual supplemental assessment requires county values, the ownership or construction event date, local rates, and proration; examples do not estimate a specific bill.

sources.title: Residential Insurance: Homeowners and Renters
sources.claimSupported: Policy shopping, common coverages and exclusions, replacement-cost concepts, FAIR Plan limits, and separate flood or earthquake considerations.
sources.limitation: The guide does not establish property-specific coverage, availability, premium, replacement cost, FAIR Plan eligibility, or insurability; the policy and licensed carrier control.

sources.title: SCR20-001 Repeal Property Tax Assessment Rates
sources.dataPeriod: Colorado property-tax calculation framework published for the 2020 session; formula verified 2026-07-13
sources.claimSupported: General relationship among actual value, assessment rate, assessed value, and local mill levies.
sources.limitation: This source supports the general formula only, not current assessment rates, exemptions, mill levies, property values, later amendments, or an actual tax bill.

sources.title: SB24-233 Property Tax
sources.dataPeriod: Enacted 2024 provisions covering property-tax years 2024 and later, including 2026
sources.claimSupported: Recent residential valuation rules, including separate treatment for school-district and other local-government levies and tax-year-specific adjustments.
sources.limitation: Later amendments and tax-year-specific rules must be read together with this act; local valuation, classification, levies, and property records control an actual bill.

sources.title: Homeowners' / Renters Insurance
sources.dataPeriod: Consumer and required coverage-summary guidance accessed 2026-07-13
sources.claimSupported: Required summary disclosures, policy controls, homeowners coverage resources, catastrophe guidance, and replacement-cost versus actual-cash-value concepts.
sources.limitation: Required summaries and consumer guidance do not replace the policy or establish property-specific coverage, exclusions, availability, premium, valuation, or claim treatment.

sources.title: Toolkit for Homeowners and HOAs on Insurance: Information and FAQs
sources.dataPeriod: Current market, policy, mitigation, and 2025 legislative guidance accessed 2026-07-13
sources.claimSupported: Insurance shopping actions, homeowners and HOA market issues, wildfire-risk transparency, mitigation questions, and deductible or owner-policy review considerations.
sources.limitation: The toolkit is general market and consumer guidance; it does not provide a quote, guarantee availability, define a property's risk, or replace policy and licensed-carrier review.

sources.title: On a mortgage, what's the difference between my principal and interest payment and my total monthly payment?
sources.claimSupported: Total mortgage payments may include taxes and insurance, while escrowed amounts can change independently of scheduled principal and interest.
sources.limitation: This explains payment components generally and does not calculate a property-specific payment, tax, premium, escrow analysis, loan term, or servicer adjustment.

sources.title: Homeowners Insurance
sources.claimSupported: Homeowners coverage, deductibles, endorsements, inspections, policy shopping, and the potential mortgage-lender insurance requirement.
sources.limitation: General state guidance does not establish property-specific coverage, exclusions, required endorsements, availability, premium, inspection result, or lender acceptance.

sources.title: Florida's Hurricane Deductible
sources.claimSupported: Hurricane-deductible options and exceptions, annual application, dollar disclosure, coverage period, and exclusion of flooding from hurricane coverage.
sources.limitation: The applicable deductible, exceptions, dollar amount, covered event, and policy treatment depend on current law and the actual policy; flooding requires separate review.

sources.title: Personal and Commercial Residential Policy Data
sources.dataPeriod: Monthly insurer reporting beginning January 2025; page accessed 2026-07-13
sources.geographicScope: Florida county and ZIP-level residential property-insurance market data
sources.claimSupported: Market Intelligence Report scope, reporting frequency, geographic detail, and OIR's notice that submitted data is not audited before publication.
sources.limitation: Insurer-submitted data are not audited by OIR; retain county or ZIP, policy or peril, reporting period, and source, and do not infer a quote or property insurability.

sources.title: Purchase Loan
sources.geographicScope: United States VA-backed home loan program
sources.claimSupported: VA purchase-loan framework covering COE, VA and lender review, occupancy, potential no-down-payment terms, mortgage-insurance treatment, and funding-fee context.
sources.limitation: Potential program benefits do not establish eligibility, approval, zero cash to close, fee treatment, rate, property acceptance, or terms for a specific borrower and transaction.

sources.title: Eligibility for VA Home Loan Programs
sources.geographicScope: United States VA direct and VA-backed home loan eligibility
sources.claimSupported: Service-period COE guidance plus the separate VA and lender credit, income, and occupancy requirements for financing.
sources.limitation: Meeting service criteria or obtaining a Certificate of Eligibility does not establish financing approval; VA and lender credit, income, occupancy, property, and transaction review remain required.

sources.title: VA Home Loan Entitlement and Limits
sources.geographicScope: United States VA home loan entitlement and guaranty calculations
sources.claimSupported: Full and remaining entitlement, lender affordability review, appraisal constraints, county-limit use, and possible down-payment implications.
sources.limitation: Entitlement alone does not determine loan amount, down payment, affordability, approval, value, or terms; prior use, appraisal, county limits, and lender review may change the scenario.

sources.title: VA Funding Fee and Loan Closing Costs
sources.geographicScope: United States VA-backed and VA direct home loans
sources.claimSupported: Funding-fee purpose, factors, exemptions, payment methods, lender-set terms, closing-cost categories, seller credits, and Loan Estimate review.
sources.limitation: The fee varies by transaction, prior use, down payment, and exemption; lenders set rates and most costs, and the reviewed schedule must be rechecked before release.

sources.title: Buying a Home With a VA-Backed Loan
sources.claimSupported: Private-lender process, VA appraisal and minimum property review, distinction from a home inspection, value options, and pre-closing disclosure review.
sources.limitation: A VA appraisal is not a home inspection or condition guarantee, and process guidance does not establish approval, value, repair outcome, timing, or lender terms.

sources.title: Conventional Loans
sources.claimSupported: General distinction between conforming and non-conforming loans and lender-to-lender variation in jumbo eligibility, pricing, and features; current numeric limits must come from FHFA.
sources.limitation: The page provides general distinctions only; current limits must come from FHFA, and lender-specific eligibility, pricing, documentation, features, and approval require direct review.

sources.title: Should I Refinance?
sources.claimSupported: Refinance goal setting, closing-cost recovery, term and payment tradeoffs, adjustable-to-fixed considerations, and use of a Loan Estimate.
sources.limitation: The 2020 handout is general education, not current pricing or a recommendation; a decision requires current complete costs, matching assumptions, borrower facts, and time horizon.

sources.title: Understanding the Costs of Refinancing
sources.claimSupported: Common refinance cost categories, points, lender comparisons, and tradeoffs behind low-cost or no-cost refinance presentations.
sources.limitation: Cost categories are educational, not a quote; actual charges, points, credits, rate, balance, and breakeven depend on current written terms and the borrower's scenario.

sources.title: Figure out how much you want to spend
sources.claimSupported: Borrower budgeting for total monthly housing cost, maintenance, utilities, emergency reserves, down payment, and closing costs.
sources.limitation: Budget guidance does not establish affordability, approval, a property-specific payment, closing costs, reserves, or suitable spending; actual household and property facts control.

sources.title: Find the right home
sources.claimSupported: Property-specific budget updates, disaster-risk and insurance review, contingencies, and home-search due diligence.
sources.limitation: The checklist does not determine a property's condition, hazards, insurance availability, value, legal status, financing eligibility, or suitable contract protections.

sources.title: The Essential Guide to Creating a Homebuying Budget
sources.claimSupported: Upfront and ongoing homeownership budget categories, including down payment, closing costs, taxes, insurance, association dues, maintenance, and reserves.
sources.limitation: The guide identifies budget categories but does not provide current loan terms, actual closing costs, property expenses, required reserves, affordability, or approval.

sources.title: Consider whether it's the right time for you to buy
sources.claimSupported: Home-value risk, repair responsibility, expected time in the home, and transaction costs involved in selling and buying.
sources.limitation: This is decision-framing guidance, not a market forecast, property valuation, cost estimate, timing recommendation, affordability result, or prediction of future home value.

sources.title: What is a home equity line of credit (HELOC)?
sources.claimSupported: HELOC draw and repayment periods, variable-rate payment risk, fees and minimums, and possible restrictions on future draws.
sources.limitation: HELOC rates, caps, fees, draw access, payment changes, eligibility, and availability vary by lender and agreement; the page is not a quote or approval.

sources.title: What is the difference between a Home Equity Loan and a Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC)?
sources.claimSupported: Lump-sum home equity loan and revolving HELOC structures, typical rate differences, and second-mortgage treatment when a first mortgage exists.
sources.limitation: The comparison describes common structures, not universal rate types, lien treatment, fees, availability, eligibility, tax results, or terms for a particular property and borrower.

sources.title: What is a home equity loan?
sources.claimSupported: Home equity loan collateral, lump-sum funding, commonly fixed rates, upfront costs, and foreclosure risk.
sources.limitation: The page describes common features and risk but does not establish a fixed rate, costs, available equity, loan amount, eligibility, approval, or suitable use for a borrower.

sources.title: What you should know about Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs)
sources.claimSupported: HELOC comparison fields for APR, index and margin, caps, fees, draw rules, repayment terms, balloon risk, disclosures, and cancellation rights.
sources.limitation: The booklet is general disclosure guidance; the lender's current agreement and disclosures control actual APR, index, margin, caps, fees, draw access, repayment, and rights.

sources.title: Does a HELOC affect my ability to refinance my first mortgage loan?
sources.claimSupported: Potential HELOC-lender approval or payoff requirement when refinancing an existing first mortgage.
sources.limitation: Whether subordination, approval, payoff, closure, or other action is required depends on the HELOC agreement, lien position, lenders, property, and proposed refinance.

sources.title: Cash-out Refinance and Paydown Behavior of Non-mortgage Debt Balances
sources.geographicScope: United States mortgage borrowers represented in the cited federal datasets
sources.claimSupported: Cash-out refinance use for debt payoff, transaction and interest costs, replacement-rate risk, equity reduction, and conversion of non-mortgage debt into home-secured debt.
sources.limitation: The report covers represented 2014-2021 borrower cohorts and does not predict an individual's behavior, savings, suitable use, current terms, credit outcome, or causal result.

sources.title: HB24B-1001: Property Tax
sources.dataPeriod: Signed 2024-09-04; provisions affecting property-tax years including 2026
sources.claimSupported: Amendments to SB24-233 residential valuation rules and the school-district limit used in the 2026 framework.
sources.limitation: This act must be read with SB24-233 and later tax-year authority; local valuation, classification, levies, exemptions, and property records control an actual bill.

sources.title: Report on the 2026 Property Tax Balancing Percentage
sources.claimSupported: No 2026 balancing reduction below the permanent 7.05% school-district residential assessment rate was required.
sources.limitation: The memorandum addresses the 2026 school-district balancing percentage only; it does not establish other levy treatment, a property's value, exemptions, or an actual bill.

sources.title: How To Get Out of Debt
sources.claimSupported: Debt-consolidation cost questions and risk of securing consolidated debt with a home.
sources.limitation: This consumer guidance does not recommend a consolidation product, calculate costs or savings, establish current terms, determine suitability, or replace legal or financial advice.
